title: DashAttention: Differentiable and Adaptive Sparse Hierarchical Attention
core_claim: DashAttention replaces fixed top-k sparse attention with an adaptive, differentiable alpha-entmax-based hierarchy that chooses a variable number of KV blocks and claims better long-context efficiency, including strong sparsity-speed tradeoffs.
triage_rationale: This looks like a technically substantive long-context attention paper with a clear algorithmic contribution and plausible systems angle, so it is worth review. The abstract is still self-asserted on key claims like non-dispersiveness and speedup, so it should be queued rather than auto-advanced.
likely_red_flags: Performance and speedup claims are only from the abstract and may depend heavily on setup
Likely incremental relative to existing hierarchical sparse attention methods
No clear evidence in the abstract of broad evaluation beyond long-context LLM settings
